mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise concurrently, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns specifically during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism reverses direction in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive equity market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, which compress valuation multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears when tested against a 3-year out-of-sample dataset.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling unemployment rates signal a synchronized recovery in construction demand and labor market health, creating a favorable earnings environment that yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising HOUST and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real gross domestic product growth reflects broad-based economic expansion that directly increases corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle expansion phases, or the predictive relationship reverses to negative during this specific regime.
